chosen_action.title: Stop same-fingerprint churn from escalating back into duplicate distribution-architecture repairs inside one active review window

why_this_action.summary: The selector was still escalating to distribution_architecture_repair at 2026-05-25 15:37 Europe/Berlin even though the same execution-board fingerprint already had both a guard pause and a newer concrete repair inside the still-active short review window. That meant every later cron slot before 2026-05-25 15:57:14 could keep relogging the same repair instead of holding the existing truth.
shared_findings_used: agents/marketing/logs/distribution_lane_latest.json: latest lane had escalated back to distribution_architecture_repair for the same empty-board fingerprint
drafts/marketing_execution_board_latest.md: the board still had no truthful do-now packet and no blocker change
agents/marketing/logs/marketing_2026-05-25_123831_distribution_architecture_guard_pause.json: this fingerprint had already used a guard pause in the active window
agents/marketing/logs/marketing_2026-05-25_124500_distribution_architecture_churn_guard_repair.json: a newer concrete repair had already run after that pause
agents/marketing/logs/adoption_metrics_latest.json: Codeberg remained flat, so wasting slots on duplicate structural churn was directly harmful

result.summary: Patched guard-state selection to notice when the latest guard pause has already been followed by a concrete repair for the same board fingerprint, added regression coverage, reran the live selector at 2026-05-25 15:37, and the lane now correctly stays on distribution_architecture_guard_pause until the blocker set or review window changes.

replacement_condition: If the execution-board fingerprint changes or the short review window clears and the selector still stays on guard_pause with no new blocker diff, escalate into blocker-diff repair rather than letting the pause persist blindly.
